ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for mochi-skin sweet xiao long bao. 'Dumpling food product' (FOODON_03315595) is the closest defensible parent class covering steamed filled dough products.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers steamed buns and dumplings as a category. No specific code exists for mochi-skin sweet xiao long bao with strawberry-white chocolate filling; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific dessert dumpling product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 10 pieces (approximately 200 g total), as sold by Din Tai Fung Singapore; each piece weighs approximately 20 g.

nutrient_source.title: Mochi (glutinous rice cake) and sweet filled dumpling nutrient profile — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for glutinous rice products, white chocolate, and strawberry jam, weighted by typical formulation ratios for mochi-skin sweet dumplings
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for steamed strawberry mochi xiao long bao. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of: (1) USDA SR Legacy #20052 — glutinous rice, cooked (mochi skin base, ~55% by weight); (2) USDA SR Legacy #19087 — white chocolate (filling fat/sugar component, ~20% by weight); (3) USDA SR Legacy #19297 — strawberry jam (filling fruit component, ~15% by weight); (4) USDA SR Legacy #18372 — wheat starch (skin binder, ~10% by weight). Composite per-100g values calculated proportionally. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of glutinous rice and mochi-type products — analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for glutinous rice (GI ~98) and white chocolate (GI ~44), weighted by carbohydrate contribution
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for steamed strawberry mochi xiao long bao. GI estimated as a carbohydrate-weighted average: glutinous rice mochi skin (GI ~98, ~60% of carbohydrate contribution) and white chocolate/strawberry jam filling (GI ~44–55, ~40% of carbohydrate contribution), yielding a blended GI of approximately 65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI 65 × carbohydrate_g per serving 95.0 g) / 100 = 61.8. Serving carbohydrate = 47.5 g per 100g × 200g serving = 95.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~42 mg per 100g; ~84 mg per 10-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: With only approximately 84 mg sodium per 10-piece serving, this dessert dumpling poses a low direct sodium burden for individuals with hypertension, though portion control remains advisable given its high sugar and caloric density.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~61.8 per 10-piece serving) driven by glutinous rice mochi skin and added sugars from strawberry jam and white chocolate filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise significant caution with this product: the glutinous rice mochi skin carries a high GI (~98 for glutinous rice), and the 10-piece serving delivers approximately 95 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~62, which will cause a substantial postprandial blood glucose rise; consumption should be limited to small portions and paired with low-GI foods if consumed at all.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from Belgian white chocolate and butter (~5.1 g per 100g; ~10.2 g per serving) and high added sugar content (~18.2 g per 100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat contribution from white chocolate and butter (~10 g per 10-piece serving) and the high added sugar load are relevant concerns for individuals with cardiovascular disease; occasional consumption in small portions is preferable, and this item should not be a regular dietary feature for those managing lipid profiles or metabolic cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Strawberry Mochi Xiao Long Bao (草莓麻糬小笼包) is a dessert variation of the classic Shanghainese xiao long bao (soup dumpling), popularised in Singapore by Din Tai Fung. Unlike the savoury pork-filled original, this version features a soft, chewy mochi (glutinous rice) skin encasing a sweet filling of homemade Korean strawberry jam blended with Belgian white chocolate, creating a ganache-like richness. The dumplings are steamed in bamboo baskets and served in a set of 10 pieces. The mochi skin provides a distinctly elastic, pillowy texture that contrasts with the warm, fruity-creamy interior. Nutritionally, the product is energy-dense (~278 kcal per 100g; ~556 kcal per 10-piece serving) and carbohydrate-dominant (~47.5 g carbohydrate per 100g), with moderate fat (~8.5 g per 100g) primarily from saturated sources in the white chocolate and butter. The glutinous rice skin has a high glycemic index (~98 for glutinous rice per University of Sydney GI Database), making the overall product a high-glycemic-load food (estimated GL ~62 per serving). Sodium is low (~42 mg per 100g), making it less concerning for hypertension from a salt perspective, but the high sugar and saturated fat content are relevant for cardiovascular and metabolic health. This item is vegetarian but not vegan (contains dairy), not certified halal, and contains gluten (wheat starch) and milk allergens. It is best consumed as an occasional dessert treat rather than a regular dietary item, particularly for individuals managing blood glucose or lipid levels.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for steamed strawberry mochi xiao long bao exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, FAO/INFOODS SEA, ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for glutinous rice, white chocolate, strawberry jam, and wheat starch. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for glutinous rice and white chocolate, weighted by carbohydrate contribution. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries; no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific product. 2. GI is an analogous estimate (blended GI ~65) based on University of Sydney GI Database values for glutinous rice (GI ~98) and white chocolate/strawberry jam (GI ~44–55), weighted by carbohydrate contribution. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 4. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 5. Halal status is listed as false because Din Tai Fung Singapore is not certified halal as of the accessed date; this should be verified against current restaurant certification. 6. The food_group has been reclassified from the input value 'Grains and staples' to 'Desserts' as this is a sweet dessert product with a mochi skin and sweet filling, more accurately categorised as a dessert item. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from the composite source model and carry medium uncertainty.
